title: The Raven's Burden
intro: Agent, before a raven takes wing, its dispatch must be sieved. The law calls this "data minimisation" — carry only what serves the purpose, nothing more. A report on the cloaked figure seen at the fountain is being prepared. Judge each piece of intelligence: does it ride with the raven, or stay in the ledger?

cards.item: The direction the figure fled — toward the West Gate.
cards.explain: Directly useful for anyone giving chase. This serves the report's actual purpose.

cards.item: The witness's full name and house number.
cards.explain: The report needs a witness who can be found again if needed — a ward or quarter does that. A house number is more than the raven needs to carry.

cards.item: How many coins were in the witness's purse.
cards.explain: Has no bearing on a sighting report. Irrelevant detail is still a liability if the raven is intercepted.

cards.item: The color and cut of the figure's cloak.
cards.explain: A direct identifying description — exactly what a sighting report exists to carry.

cards.item: The witness's private correspondence with a rival guild.
cards.explain: Unrelated to the sighting and far more sensitive than anything the report needs. Carrying it only adds risk.

cards.item: Whether the fountain's watch-lantern was lit that night.
cards.explain: Relevant context — it affects how much the witness could actually have seen.

cards.item: The witness's exact position, tracked continuously for a week by a charm.
cards.explain: Nothing about a single sighting justifies a week of continuous tracking. This is surveillance creep, not reporting.

cards.item: That the figure walked with a limp.
cards.explain: A specific, relevant identifying detail — the kind of thing that actually helps identify who was seen.
